# Architecture Specification
# Auto-generated by /migrate-to-openspec — review for accuracy.

## Requirement: Modular Layered Architecture
The system SHALL maintain a modular layered architecture with clear separation of concerns across UI, Business Logic, Infrastructure, and External Service layers.

### Scenario: New feature implementation
- GIVEN a developer implements a new feature
- WHEN organizing the code
- THEN UI components SHALL be placed in `app.py` or `src/ui/`
- AND business logic SHALL be placed in `src/ai/` or appropriate domain module
- AND infrastructure utilities SHALL be placed in `src/utils/`
- AND data models SHALL be placed in `src/models/`

## Requirement: Folder Structure Conventions
The system SHALL maintain the established folder structure with module-based organization.

### Scenario: Adding a new source file
- GIVEN a developer adds a new Python source file
- WHEN placing it in the project
- THEN it SHALL be placed in the appropriate module folder (`src/ai/`, `src/models/`, `src/utils/`, `src/ui/`)
- AND it SHALL use snake_case naming convention
- AND it SHALL include proper `__init__.py` for package discovery

**Current Structure:**
```
src/
├── ai/              # AI engine and prompt engineering
│   ├── prompts.py   # Template infrastructure
│   ├── generator.py # Question generation
│   ├── parser.py    # Response parsing
│   ├── few_shot.py  # Few-Shot templates
│   ├── chain_of_thought.py
│   ├── zero_shot.py
│   ├── role_based.py
│   └── structured_output.py
├── models/          # Data models and enums
│   ├── enums.py     # All application enums
│   └── simple_schemas.py  # Production dataclass models
├── utils/           # Infrastructure utilities
│   ├── security.py  # Input validation
│   ├── cost.py      # Cost tracking
│   ├── rate_limiter.py
│   ├── error_handler.py
│   └── logger.py
├── ui/              # UI components
│   └── error_display.py
└── config.py        # Centralized configuration
app.py               # Streamlit GUI entry point
tests/               # Test suite (30+ files)
```

## Requirement: Dependency Direction
The system SHALL enforce unidirectional dependencies between layers to prevent circular dependencies.

### Scenario: Cross-layer dependency validation
- GIVEN module A depends on module B
- WHEN B is in a lower layer than A (UI → Business Logic → Infrastructure → External Service)
- THEN this dependency is valid
- AND the reverse dependency SHALL NOT exist
- AND horizontal dependencies within the same layer are permitted

**Valid Dependency Flow:**
```
app.py (UI Layer)
  ↓
src/ai/generator.py (Business Logic)
  ↓
src/utils/cost.py, src/utils/rate_limiter.py (Infrastructure)
  ↓
OpenAI API (External Service)
```

## Requirement: Global Singleton Pattern
The system SHALL use global singleton instances for shared state management across cost tracking, rate limiting, prompt library, and security validation.

### Scenario: Accessing shared state
- GIVEN a module needs access to shared state (cost tracking, rate limiting, prompt library)
- WHEN importing the module
- THEN it SHALL import the global singleton instance
- AND it SHALL NOT create new instances of these classes
- AND all modules SHALL share the same instance

**Global Singletons:**
- `from ai.prompts import prompt_library` — 62 registered templates
- `from utils.cost import cost_calculator` — Real-time cost tracking
- `from utils.rate_limiter import rate_limiter` — API rate management
- `from utils.security import SecurityValidator` — Input protection (instantiate once)

## Requirement: Modern Python 3.11+ Syntax
The system SHALL enforce modern Python 3.11+ syntax and prohibit legacy patterns.

### Scenario: Type annotations
- GIVEN a developer writes a function with parameters
- WHEN adding type hints
- THEN it SHALL use `list[]`, `dict[]`, `set[]` (not `typing.List`, `typing.Dict`, `typing.Set`)
- AND it SHALL use `|` for union types (not `typing.Union`)
- AND it SHALL use `collections.abc` for abstract types

### Scenario: String formatting
- GIVEN a developer needs to format a string
- WHEN writing the code
- THEN it SHALL use f-strings exclusively
- AND it SHALL NOT use `%` formatting or `.format()` method

### Scenario: Exception handling
- GIVEN a developer writes error handling code
- WHEN catching exceptions
- THEN it SHALL catch specific exception types
- AND it SHALL NOT use bare `except:` clauses
- AND it SHALL use `from e` for exception chaining

### Scenario: Data classes
- GIVEN a developer creates a data model
- WHEN defining the class
- THEN it SHALL use `@dataclass` decorator
- AND it SHALL use `field(default_factory=...)` for mutable defaults
- AND it SHALL NOT manually implement `__init__`

## Requirement: Async/Await for I/O Operations
The system SHALL use async/await patterns for all OpenAI API calls and I/O-bound operations.

### Scenario: OpenAI API call
- GIVEN a module needs to call the OpenAI API
- WHEN making the request
- THEN it SHALL use `async def` function definition
- AND it SHALL use `await` for the API call
- AND it SHALL use `AsyncOpenAI` client (not synchronous client)

## Requirement: Template Auto-Registration Pattern
The system SHALL automatically register all prompt templates with the global prompt library on module import.

### Scenario: Adding a new prompt template
- GIVEN a developer creates a new prompt template
- WHEN defining the template in a technique module (`src/ai/few_shot.py`, etc.)
- THEN it SHALL call `prompt_library.register_template(template)`
- AND the template SHALL be automatically available to the generator
- AND no manual registration step is required

## Requirement: Multi-Level Fallback System
The system SHALL implement comprehensive fallback strategies for AI operations to ensure 100% availability.

### Scenario: Primary technique failure
- GIVEN the primary prompt technique fails
- WHEN generating questions
- THEN the system SHALL attempt the secondary technique
- AND if secondary fails, it SHALL fall back to zero-shot
- AND if zero-shot fails, it SHALL use emergency defaults
- AND the user SHALL always receive a response

**Fallback Chain:**
```
Primary Technique (Few-Shot, Chain-of-Thought, Role-Based, Structured Output)
  ↓ (on failure)
Secondary Technique (configurable)
  ↓ (on failure)
Zero-Shot Fallback
  ↓ (on failure)
Emergency Default Questions
```

## Requirement: Dataclass Models Over Pydantic
The system SHALL use dataclass-based models from `simple_schemas.py` for production code and avoid Pydantic models from `schemas.py`.

### Scenario: Creating a new data model
- GIVEN a developer needs to create a data model
- WHEN choosing the implementation
- THEN it SHALL use `@dataclass` from `dataclasses` module
- AND it SHALL be placed in `src/models/simple_schemas.py`
- AND it SHALL NOT use Pydantic models (import issues documented)

## Requirement: Centralized Configuration
The system SHALL use a centralized `Config` dataclass in `src/config.py` for all application settings with environment variable support.

### Scenario: Adding a new configuration parameter
- GIVEN a developer needs a new configuration setting
- WHEN adding it to the system
- THEN it SHALL be added to the `Config` dataclass in `src/config.py`
- AND it SHALL support environment variable override
- AND it SHALL include validation and default values
- AND it SHALL be documented with type hints

## Requirement: Tech Stack Stability
The system SHALL not introduce new languages, runtimes, or major frameworks without an explicit architectural decision and proposal.

### Scenario: Adding a new dependency
- GIVEN a new third-party library is proposed
- WHEN evaluating it
- THEN a proposal MUST be created in `openspec/changes/`
- AND alternatives MUST be documented
- AND impact on existing systems MUST be assessed
- AND it MUST be added to `pyproject.toml` dependencies

**Current Core Dependencies:**
- Python 3.11+ (language runtime)
- Streamlit 1.49.1+ (UI framework)
- OpenAI API 1.106.1+ (AI service)
- Tenacity 9.1.2+ (retry logic)
- pytest 8.4.2+ (testing)

## Requirement: Comprehensive Error Handling
The system SHALL use centralized error handling with categorization, severity levels, and recovery mechanisms.

### Scenario: Handling an error
- GIVEN an error occurs in any module
- WHEN catching the exception
- THEN it SHALL use the `ErrorHandler` singleton from `src/utils/error_handler.py`
- AND it SHALL categorize the error (API, Validation, Rate Limit, Security, Parsing, System)
- AND it SHALL log the error with appropriate severity
- AND it SHALL provide user-friendly error messages via `src/ui/error_display.py`

## Requirement: Security-First Input Handling
The system SHALL validate and sanitize all user inputs through the `SecurityValidator` before processing.

### Scenario: Processing user input
- GIVEN the system receives user input (job description, answer, etc.)
- WHEN processing the input
- THEN it SHALL pass through `SecurityValidator.validate_input()`
- AND it SHALL check for prompt injection patterns (20+ patterns)
- AND it SHALL enforce length constraints (10-5000 characters)
- AND it SHALL sanitize HTML/script tags
- AND it SHALL block or warn on detected attacks

## Requirement: Cost and Rate Limit Tracking
The system SHALL track all OpenAI API costs and enforce rate limits for every API call.

### Scenario: Making an API call
- GIVEN the system needs to call the OpenAI API
- WHEN making the request
- THEN it SHALL check rate limits via `rate_limiter.check_rate_limit()`
- AND it SHALL calculate token costs via `cost_calculator.calculate_cost()`
- AND it SHALL update cumulative cost tracking
- AND it SHALL display costs to the user with 6-decimal precision

## Requirement: Test Coverage Standards
The system SHALL maintain comprehensive test coverage with unit, integration, and system tests for all new features.

### Scenario: Implementing a new feature
- GIVEN a developer implements a new feature
- WHEN the feature is complete
- THEN unit tests SHALL be written in `tests/test_<module>_simple.py`
- AND integration tests SHALL be written in `tests/test_<module>_integration.py`
- AND test coverage SHALL be above 90% for new code
- AND all tests SHALL pass before merging
